userGroups.dailyValueTargetGroupName: Adults and children 4 or more years of age
userGroups.langualCodeDescription: Adults and Children 4 years and above

activeIngredients.name: Vitamin B12
activeIngredients.notes: Vitamin B12 (Form: as Methylcobalamin) Note: 5,000 mcg

statements.notes:
Guaranteed Quality
This is an FDA-regulated dietary supplement that is produced in an FDA- and third party-inspected facility. We are certified for producing dietary supplements and USDA Organic products using Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP). All ingredients are quality tested to meet or exceed specifications. Our quality team goes above and beyond standard quality control to ensure the safety and efficacy of this supplement. We utilize advanced analytical and microbiological testing methods, with capabilities that are state-of-the-art and exceed basic lab requirements.
This product contains an Ageless(R) anti-oxygen packet to enhance protection during storage.
SUGGESTED USAGE: As a dietary supplement, take one lozenge 1 to 2 times daily. Chew lozenge or hold in mouth until dissolved.

Vitamin B12 is an essential nutrient that cannot by synthesized by animals or plants, only by micro-organisms. Most of B12's benefits are related to its co-enzyme activity, such as enzymes needed for the production of DNA during cell division . It is also necessary for the methylation of homocysteine, a harmful by-product of protein metabolism. B12 is necessary for the maintenance of a healthy nervous system. Methylcobalamin is better absorbed and retained than other forms of B12.
These statements have not been evaluated by the FDA. This product is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure or prevent any disease.

- Advanced CoEnzyme Form
- Homocysteine Regulator
10% of profits donated for health research
Contains no sugar, salt, starch, yeast, wheat, gluten, soy, milk, egg, shellfish, or preservatives.
Do Not Eat Freshness Packet. Keep in Bottle.
Store in a cool, dry place.

Vitamin B12 (often referred to as cobalamin) is an essential nutrient. It is critical for nervous system function, assists in the production of red blood cells, and is important in DNA and RNA production (the genetic material).
Vitamin B12 is naturally found in foods that come from animals, including fish, meat, poultry, eggs, milk, and milk products.
Protocol For Life Balance(TM) Vitamin B12 is provided in the biologically active, or coenzyme form to maximize the body's utilization of this product.

Cautions and Interactions
Generally well tolerated even at high levels of intake.
